violations.text: The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was >400 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quat sanitizer is 200 ppm. PIC added additional water. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - slicer, - kitchen storage shelves, - table under prep. - Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Bar mats were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed bar mats. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

The hand-washing sink on the cook line was blocked by a trashcan. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. PIC relocated trashcan. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

Shellfish tags were lacking last used date. Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC notified. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

The following food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils were observed with an accumulation of food and debris: - can opener, - ice machine (ice discarded), - immersion blender, - slicer, - plates. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took items out of service to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher (bar AND small kitchen dishwashers) were 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service during inspection. Service arrived during inspection. Establishment to use large dishwasher or 3 bay sink to sanitize dishes. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

Various items, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

Tomato soup was observed held between 117-120 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

Chicken, which was stacked above the fill line of a prep unit, was observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in freezer for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

Stainless steel cleaner was stored with equipment and utensils. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. PIC removed chemical. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025
Utensils were observed stored in a containers of sanitizer. PIC removed and placed items in 3 bay sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025
Establishment par cooks racks of lamb and does not have an approved variance for par cooking. PIC to stop par cooking until approved by DOH-CFP. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, sauces and stocks were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025
Container of in-use utensils were stored on the floor on the cook line. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen area. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris:- prep units,- table under grill,- sides of cooking appliances. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025
Food prep sink located on cook line was lacking an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Observed open container of breadcrumbs being store under chemicals on a chemical storage shelves. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored on food storage shelves in back storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Inspector shelled raw oysters being stored in water in the cookline prep unit.PIC voluntarily disposed of food product and informed of proper procedures for storing shelled seafood. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the kitchen glass dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Various food product was observed held at (46-50 ) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024
ROP lobster observed at 46df with package intact. PIC voluntarily disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
A pan and colander was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar and kitchen glass dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service call was made during inspection New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black and pink residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice disposed. Unit taken out of service to be wash and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Various food product was observed held at (47-54) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
Thermometers in refrigeration units observed in poor repair. Inaccurate ambient temperature readings. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
Bags of bread were observed stored on the floor in the (back storage area). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Serving utensils and knives was observed being stored between prep units During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Utensils and knives were taken to warewashing area to be wash and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Lowboy refrigerator unit located in the (bar) area was not in good repair. Observed ambient temperatures at 50DF.Establsihment not allowed to store any TCS food product in unit repaired/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
Prep Unit hood handles located in the (cookline) area was not in good repair. Observed missing door handles New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (chlorine) sanitizing solution for the (glass dishwashers). New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
